cells.xml_text_content: Table 6: Proportion of employed people working in a high-skilled occupation, by religion, England and Wales, 2012 to 2018
cells.pdf_text_content: Table 6: Proportion of employed people working in a high-skilled occupation, by religion, England and Wales, 2012 to 2018

cells.xml_text_content: Applies to people aged 16 to 64 in employment.
cells.pdf_text_content: Applies to people aged 16 to 64 in employment.

cells.xml_text_content: The skill level groups are created by grouping jobs together based on their occupation according to the Standard Occupation Classification (SOC) 2010 lower level groups.
cells.pdf_text_content: The skill level groups are created by grouping jobs together based on their occupation according to the Standard Occupation Classification (SOC) 2010 lower level groups.

cells.xml_text_content: Occupations were grouped by the skill level required according to the following guidelines:
cells.pdf_text_content: Occupations were grouped by the skill level required according to the following guidelines:

cells.xml_text_content: High – This skill level is normally acquired through a degree or an equivalent period of work experience. Occupations at this level are generally termed ‘professional’
cells.pdf_text_content: High – This skill level is normally acquired through a degree or an equivalent period of work experience. Occupations at this level are generally termed ‘professional’

cells.xml_text_content: or managerial positions, and are found in corporate enterprises or governments. Examples include senior government officials, financial managers,
cells.pdf_text_content: or managerial positions, and are found in corporate enterprises or governments. Examples include senior government officials, financial managers,

cells.xml_text_content: scientists, engineers, medical doctors, teachers and accountants.
cells.pdf_text_content: scientists, engineers, medical doctors, teachers and accountants.

cells.xml_text_content: High skill level data can be found in table 6, low skill level data can be found in table 7, upper-middle skill level data can be found in table 8 and lower-middle skill level data can be found in table 9.
cells.pdf_text_content: High skill level data can be found in table 6, low skill level data can be found in table 7, upper-middle skill level data can be found in table 8 and lower-middle skill level data can be found in table 9.

cells.xml_text_content: Note that the total percentages of high, low, upper-middle and lower-middle skill levels may not sum to 100 as missing values are included in totals.
cells.pdf_text_content: Note that the total percentages of high, low, upper-middle and lower-middle skill levels may not sum to 100 as missing values are included in totals.
